angle: A4 -- gravitational slip eta=Psi/Phi from the Skyrmion anisotropic stress
engine: MG1 BD relaxer + su3_core Skyrmion (E2 sigma-sector stress)
verdict: the DEV slip [2.2%,4.1%] is NOT reproduced as a robust feature: the interior slip is O(1) (|eta-1|_int=0.31), as expected for a RELATIVISTIC soliton -- not a 2-4% galactic plateau; the exterior is a CONSTANT slip plateau |eta-1|~61% (the anisotropic stress has a non-zero monopole, int Pi/int eps=-0.61) -- a genuine slip in FORM, but O(1), ~15x the DEV window; the orientation tilt <n>(r)~r^-5.1 and Phi(r)~r^-0.9 have DIFFERENT falloffs, so <n>/theta is not the constant ratio of a DEV slip.  The DEV slip is a galactic vector-sector (A_mu) effect, and A1 showed A_mu does not emerge -> eta stays [EXTERNO-B]/[INCONCLUSIVO].
caveat: sigma(E2)-sector stress used (rigorous, exterior-dominant); E4/Skyrme anisotropic stress omitted as a declared subleading correction.  This is the LOCAL matter-sourced slip of a baryon, conceptually related but not identical to the DEV's effective galactic slip.
anti_circularity: DEV window only in COMPARISON block; slip from F(r)+BD relaxer
